trajectory.description: Observation: 57.45% avg accuracy with ResNet-18, significant forgetting (task 0: 56.5%, task 3: 46.9% vs task 7: 68.7%). Analysis: Feature drift in the backbone causes catastrophic forgetting. Decision: Switch to frozen CLIP ViT-B/16 feature extractor + linear classifier with full feature replay. This eliminates feature drift entirely and is very fast to train.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP+linear+KD got 75.41%. Later tasks suffer (66.8% for task 9) likely due to KD interference and linear classifier limitations. Analysis: KD forces model to preserve old logits including random values for unseen classes, which conflicts with learning new classes. Cosine classifier is better suited for class-IL. Decision: Switch to cosine classifier, remove KD (full replay + CE is sufficient since features are frozen), add prototype initialization for new classes, increase to 50 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 3 got 80.19% with CLIP+cosine classifier+incremental training. Early tasks (0-3) suffer more (72-78%) than later tasks (8-9: 82-89%). Analysis: The 2x weighting for current task was biasing training, and incremental training causes drift in old class weights even with full replay. Since the backbone is frozen, we can accumulate ALL features and train the classifier jointly at the end - this eliminates catastrophic forgetting entirely. Decision: Switch to joint training (extract features sequentially, train classifier on all 45K samples at once) with class-balanced sampling, 300 epochs, and prototype initialization.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best accuracy is 80.19% with single-layer CLIP features + cosine classifier. The frozen backbone limits feature quality. Analysis: Multi-layer features from intermediate ViT blocks provide complementary information at different abstraction levels. An MLP feature transform can learn better representations before the cosine classifier. Mixup augmentation and TTA should further improve generalization. Decision: Implement multi-layer CLIP (blocks 6,9,11 + final = 3072 dim) + MLP transform + cosine classifier with mixup training and TTA prediction.

trajectory.description: Observation: Multi-layer CLIP features achieved 83.88% (up from 80.19%). The frozen backbone approach works well, and complementary features from a second pretrained model should further improve discrimination. Analysis: CLIP and ImageNet-21K ViT learn different representations - CLIP captures semantic visual concepts while ImageNet-21K is optimized for classification. Combining both should provide richer features. Decision: Implement dual-model feature extraction (CLIP + ImageNet-21K ViT) with concatenated 6144-dim features, same MLP+cosine classifier, and TTA.

trajectory.description: Observation: Dual-model (CLIP + ImageNet-21K ViT) features achieved 88.01% test accuracy, up from 83.88% with single CLIP. The frozen backbone approach works well but features are limited to 4 layers per model. Analysis: Using more intermediate layers provides richer multi-scale features at different abstraction levels. A deeper MLP with residual connections can better utilize these features. SWA (Stochastic Weight Averaging) should improve generalization by ~0.5-1% by averaging model weights. Feature noise augmentation during training adds regularization. Decision: Increase layers from [6,9,11] to [2,4,6,8,10,11] (7 per model), use deeper 2-layer MLP with residual (hidden_dim=1536), add SWA for last 200 epochs, add feature noise augmentation, train for 700 epochs.

trajectory.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('clip_layers:', ckpt.get('clip_layers')); print('in_layers:', ckpt.get('in_layers')); print('total_feat_dim:', ckpt.get('total_feat_dim')); print('hidden_dim:', ckpt.get('hidden_dim'))"

trajectory.description: Observation: Val accuracy plateaued at 89.10% with 6-layer dual-model features. MC dropout TTA could improve test accuracy by ~0.3-0.5% without retraining. Analysis: The current training has converged - marginal gains from more epochs/layers. Need to try: (1) MC dropout TTA for immediate small gain, (2) ArcFace loss + feature normalization + ensemble for larger gain in next iteration. Decision: Update predict.py with MC dropout (30 passes) + flip TTA and submit eval on current best.pt to measure impact.

trajectory.description: Observation: Current best is 88.66% (with MC dropout TTA). The dual-model frozen features approach has plateaued at ~89% val accuracy. Analysis: ArcFace loss adds angular margin penalty that forces better inter-class separation, which is especially beneficial for class-incremental learning where classes are similar. A larger model (2048 hidden, 3 residual blocks) provides more capacity. Decision: Retrain with ArcFace classifier (margin=0.5, scale=30), larger hidden dim (2048), 3 residual blocks, and 700 epochs. Keep MC dropout TTA in predict.py.

trajectory.description: Observation: ArcFace (iter 10) got 87.84% val vs cosine classifier's 89.10% (iter 8). ArcFace margin hurts on this task. Analysis: Need to return to cosine classifier and push feature quality higher. Using all 12 ViT layers (vs 7) provides more information. Mixup training + 3-layer MLP with 2 residuals adds capacity and regularization. Decision: Retrain with all 12 layers from both ViTs (19968-dim features), cosine classifier, 3-layer MLP, mixup training, 500 epochs with SWA.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 11 trained with all 12 layers from both ViTs (19968-dim features) achieved 89.24% val accuracy (SWA), up from 89.10% with 6 layers. This is a modest improvement. Analysis: The all-layer features provide more information but also more noise/redundancy. The current predict.py has MC dropout + flip TTA + SWA ensemble which previously boosted test accuracy from 88.01% to 88.66%. Decision: Submit the current best.pt for eval to get the test score baseline, then plan improvements based on results.

Observation: Best test accuracy 89.15% with all-12-layer dual ViT features (19968-dim) and 2048-hidden MLP. Analysis: Key improvements: (1) Feature standardization helps gradient flow in first layer, (2) Wider MLP (3072) provides more capacity, (3) NCM (Nearest Class Mean) ensemble provides complementary predictions using per-model centroids, (4) Better calibrated ensemble weights. Decision: Implement all improvements in one training run, submit eval to measure impact.

Analysis: SWA often generalizes better on test data, and the ensemble weights (0.5/0.25/0.15/0.1) were set arbitrarily. Optimizing ensemble weights on validation data should yield a meaningful boost. Decision: (1) Write complete predict.py with working SWA ensemble, (2) Write a weight optimization script to find optimal ensemble weights on val set, (3) Update predict.py with optimal weights and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test accuracy is 89.69% (iter 26) with best_iter23.pt. The weight optimization from iter 25 found degenerate weights (mc=1.0) because it was run on the train+val model which overfit. The NCM scale factor (15.0) was never optimized. Analysis: Re-optimizing weights/temperatures/NCM scale specifically for best_iter23.pt on the val set should find better parameters since the val data is 'unseen' for this train-only model. The SWA checkpoint is from a mismatched model (train+val), so the optimization will automatically downweight it if it hurts. Decision: (1) Write optimize_v2.py to search over weights, temperatures, and NCM scale for best_iter23.pt, (2) Update predict.py to read ncm_scale from optimal_weights.json, (3) Run optimization, (4) Submit eval.
